methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Runway
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Magic AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B+

data.narrative:
Luma AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Amit Jain and Alex Yu, is currently valued at $4.0B post-money on $1.07B raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Figure AI, Skild AI, Runway, Magic AI) alongside limited public comparables.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x Series C-to-next-stage step-up multiplier applied to the $4.0B base, implies a forward valuation of $6.8B. Peer-median benchmarking across the 4 comparables yields $7.8B, though the range is wide: $1.5B floor (Runway, Magic AI) to $39.0B ceiling (Figure AI). This dispersion reflects substantial variance in perceived value creation across frontier AI companies at equivalent funding stages.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $5.8B (low) to $8.9B (high), with a median implied value of $7.3B. This suggests the current $4.0B valuation sits 30–55% below the median comparable multiple, though within the observed peer distribution. Key uncertainties include the explanatory factors driving the 26x spread between lowest and highest peer valuations, the representativeness of a 4-company peer set, and the trajectory assumptions embedded in stage-progression multiples during the current AI funding cycle.
